content.full_text:
The Municipal Court

NORTH COUNTY JUDICIAL DISTRICT
328 SOUTH MELROSE DRIVE
VISTA, CA 92083-6893

MARGUERITE L. WAGNER
JUDGE

April 13, 1992


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Sir or Madame:

Sharon Lear, the Assistant Court Administrator for the North County Municipal Court spoke with Wayne Imberie of your office on April 9, 1992. In that conversation, she made inquiry as to whether or not a person need report gifts in the form of entertainment or otherwise arising from a dating relationship.

Mr. Imberie stated that so long as the gifts emanated from a "bonafide" dating relationship, said gifts would be exempt from the reporting requirements.

Apparently, there is a letter from 1989 addressing this issue and, if I specifically requested same, it would be forwarded to me. I am making a formal request for that letter so that I will be protected in the future if there are any questions pertaining to reporting of gifts under this situation.

If you have any questions please contact me or Ms. Lear at the above address or telephone number.

Very truly yours,

Marguerite L. Wagner, Judge
North County Municipal Court

ML/rjc

cc: Ms. Sharon Lear

sections.question_synthetic: The inquiry concerns whether gifts from a bonafide dating relationship must be reported under FPPC gift disclosure requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request document, not an FPPC response letter. The document references a prior FPPC phone consultation and requests documentation of that advice. No formal question/conclusion sections present in this incoming correspondence format.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from a North County Municipal Court Judge requesting a copy of a 1989 FPPC advice letter regarding the exemption from gift reporting requirements for gifts arising from bonafide dating relationships, following a phone conversation between court staff and FPPC staff.
